close_reason: Made ?algorithm= optional with auto-detection via detect_key() PKCS#8 trial decoding
description:
secretx-aws-kms accepts no algorithm param and defaults to ecdsa-p256 (aws-kms lib.rs:74-82). secretx-local-signing requires the algorithm param (lib.rs:87-91). A user reading the workspace may reasonably expect either the same default or the same requirement.

Defensible status quo: local-signing CANNOT default the algorithm because the PKCS#8 DER file alone does not encode a single canonical interpretation that matches across Ed25519/P-256/RSA-PSS choice (RSA keys for example can be used with PKCS#1-v1.5 OR PSS). AWS-KMS can default because the key's algorithm is part of its KMS metadata.

Recommendation: keep the mandatory-param status quo, but add a doc paragraph in the URI section that explicitly cross-references AWS-KMS and explains WHY local-signing cannot default. This avoids the next reader filing a 'make this consistent' bug.
title: Mandatory ?algorithm= param is inconsistent with secretx-aws-kms (defaultable); cross-crate UX drift
